content:
Long story short: I'm currently trying to write a set of bindings for modesetting in DRM for rust. I think it's probably easiest if I first link to the context of this question rather then trying to type it out again:

https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/415254-DRM/topic/More.20KMS.20bindings.20feedback.2Fquestions

Given that context - I have found no way of creating an Arc<UnsafeCell<Plane<T>>>. The furthest I've managed to get is this:

        let plane: Arc<Plane<T>> = Arc::try_new(try_init!(
            Self {
                plane: bindings::drm_plane { ..Default::default() },
                inner <- T::new(),
                _p: PhantomPinned,
            }
        ));

for the life of me I cannot figure out how on earth I would make an UnsafeCell<> here. Or even if that's the thing that I actually want to do? I, think it is? I'd extremely appreciate any help (or alternative ideas if the way I'm trying to implement this is just silly)
Do you need an UnsafeCell wrapper around Plane since Plane already contains one?

UnsafeCell is a low level primitive and should not be directly used. Also note that UnsafeCell is not Sync, so you will not be able to share Arc<UnsafeCell<T>> between threads, making it rather pointless to use an Arc.
